findings.evidence: Lines 136-140: docstring claims continuous spatial kernels; signature only includes `Continuous (fun v : V => F (t, v))`.
findings.fix: Rewrite the docstring to say it packages the positive-definite kernel with continuity of the fixed-time slice, or strengthen the theorem with the needed topological group assumptions and prove kernel continuity.
findings.issue: The docstring says a continuous semigroup-group PD function has “continuous positive-definite spatial kernels”, but the theorem proves `IsPositiveDefiniteKernel (fun v w => F (t, v - w)) ∧ Continuous (fun v => F (t, v))`, not continuity of the two-variable kernel.

summary: One public theorem docstring overclaims the result by documenting continuity of the spatial kernel, while the theorem only returns continuity of the one-variable slice.
